name: baton-mem
description: A local, auditable session-memory layer for VS Code and GitHub Copilot, built for engineers who need persistent project context without relying on a cloud memory backend.

commands.title: Baton: Inject Relevant Context Into Copilot Chat...
Baton: Show External MCP Client Config
Baton: Export Session as Diff-Friendly Markdown...

chatParticipants.name: baton
chatParticipants.description: Query persistent coding session memory with progressive disclosure

commands.name: health
commands.description: Show memory health score and notes

commands.name: search
commands.description: Search past sessions for a keyword/query

commands.name: timeline
commands.description: Show chronological sessions around a date/query

commands.name: detail
commands.description: Get full detail of a session by ID prefix

commands.name: recent
commands.description: Show the most recent sessions

commands.name: azure
commands.description: Show Azure-tagged sessions grouped by subsystem

commands.name: export
commands.description: Export a session (by ID prefix) as diff-friendly markdown

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show memory store stats

commands.name: standup
commands.description: Generate an AI daily standup note from yesterday's sessions

commands.name: commit
commands.description: Generate an AI conventional commit message from staged git changes + sessions

commands.name: ask
commands.description: Ask a question about your coding history (RAG over sessions)

commands.name: recap
commands.description: Generate a narrative weekly/monthly recap of your coding sessions

commands.name: savings
commands.description: Show token savings breakdown — compression ratio, lifetime totals, and dollar-equivalent estimates

commands.name: related
commands.description: Find all sessions that touched the currently open file — zero typing needed

commands.name: decisions
commands.description: Export architecture decisions as an ADR-style doc, optionally filtered by keyword

commands.name: whereami
commands.description: Interruption recovery brief — what you were working on, where you left off, and your next step

commands.name: debt
commands.description: Technical debt ledger — surface TODO/FIXME/HACK signals from session history, grouped by age

commands.name: adr
commands.description: Auto-generate a formal Architecture Decision Record from session history, optionally filtered by topic

commands.name: pr
commands.description: PR review context — surface sessions matching a PR's changed files (pass branch name or PR number)

commands.name: precommit
commands.description: Pre-commit architectural consistency check — verify staged changes against past decisions before committing

commands.name: audit
commands.description: Workspace integrity audit — catch version drift and cross-file inconsistencies (e.g. README footer vs package.json)

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Re-run grounding validation on a session by ID prefix — shows verified / drifted / missing files

commands.name: correct
commands.description: Correct a session by ID prefix — captures a new corrected note, links it to the original, and supersedes it

commands.name: supersede
commands.description: Mark one session as superseding another (usage: /supersede <newerId> <olderId>)

commands.name: retract
commands.description: Retract a session by ID prefix — excludes it from retrieval and injection (use 'undo' to reverse)

commands.name: accept
commands.description: Mark a retrieved session as useful — strengthens its retrieval reinforcement signal

commands.name: reject
commands.description: Mark a retrieved session as unhelpful — weakens its retrieval reinforcement signal

commands.name: entity
commands.description: Aggregate every session that touched a file or symbol (usage: /entity <path> or /entity <path>#<symbol>)

commands.name: snippet
commands.description: Chunk-level retrieval — returns matching decisions/problems/summaries/topics across all sessions

commands.name: conflicts
commands.description: List pending conflict warnings — sessions whose new decisions may contradict older ones

commands.name: lineage
commands.description: Show cross-session causal chain (predecessors + successors) for a session by ID prefix

commands.name: why
commands.description: Score-decomposition explainer — break down why a session ranked where it did (usage: /why <query> :: <id>)

commands.name: graph
commands.description: Emit a Mermaid flowchart of the decision graph (supersession + correction + causal edges); optional file: filter

commands.name: compliance
commands.description: Audit-friendly posture report: grounding coverage, trust distribution, conflict counts, redaction stats, custom-entity inventory

commands.name: route
commands.description: Context-acquisition recommender — given a question, returns whether MCP tools or file open is cheaper and estimates token cost for each path

languageModelTools.name: baton_search
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search the user's persistent Baton coding session memory for past decisions, problems solved, files touched, and topics. Use this BEFORE asking the user for repo context that might already be known. Returns a compact list of matching sessions with IDs, summaries, files, and decisions.

sinceDays.description: Only return sessions from the last N days.

workspaceOnly.description: Limit results to the current workspace (default true).

languageModelTools.name: baton_store
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Persist a short, self-contained note into Baton so it will be recalled in future sessions. Use when the user states a durable fact, decision, or preference the assistant should remember. Do NOT use for ephemeral chat context.

summary.description: 2-4 sentence summary of what should be remembered.

languageModelTools.name: baton_audit
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run Baton's workspace integrity audit. Catches cross-file inconsistencies that erode release trust — most importantly, version drift between package.json, README footer, docs/DEMO.md, and CHANGELOG.md. Returns a list of errors and warnings with the offending file, line, and a suggested fix. Read-only — does not modify any file or memory.

rules.description: Optional subset of rules to run (e.g. ['version-drift']). Empty/omitted = run all.

baton.compressionIntervalMinutes.description: How often to compress captured events (minutes)

baton.maxStoredSessions.description: Maximum number of compressed sessions to retain

baton.maxStoreSizeMB.markdownDescription: Soft cap on the on-disk size of `~/.baton-mem/sessions.json`. When exceeded, oldest sessions are evicted until under cap (count-based `#baton.maxStoredSessions#` and age-based `#baton.retentionDays#` still apply first).

baton.retentionDays.description: Delete sessions older than this many days (0 = disabled)

baton.enterpriseMode.markdownDescription: Strict mode for regulated environments. Disables terminal capture, raw snippets, team export, and MCP write tools, and stores only redacted summaries.

baton.captureCodeSnippets.markdownDescription: Store a short redacted snippet for file edits. Disabled automatically in enterprise mode.

baton.allowMcpWriteAccess.markdownDescription: Allow MCP write tools (memory-store, memory-delete, memory-correct, memory-retract, memory-supersede). **Default `false`** — MCP exposes read-only retrieval (search, recent, timeline, get) until you explicitly opt in. Disabled automatically in enterprise mode.

baton.allowTeamExport.markdownDescription: Allow team export and memory-pack export flows. Disabled automatically in enterprise mode.

baton.previewBeforePersist.markdownDescription: Show a preview before a compressed memory snapshot is persisted.

baton.contextRetrievalCount.description: Number of relevant past sessions to inject

baton.redactSecrets.description: Scan and redact secrets/PII before storing

baton.excludeGlobs.description: Glob patterns whose file events will be skipped

baton.autoInjectStartupContext.description: Write .github/instructions/session-memory.instructions.md on every VS Code startup and after each compression so Copilot always has recent session context.

baton.startupContextSessionCount.description: Number of recent sessions to include in the auto-injected instructions file.

baton.idleTimeoutSeconds.description: Seconds of editor inactivity before idle-triggered compression fires (0 = disabled)

flags.description: Regex flags (g, i, m, etc.). Defaults to 'g'

baton.customRedactionRules.description: User-defined regex redaction rules applied after the built-in 26-rule set (enterprise compliance modes)

baton.customSensitiveEntities.description: Plain-string entity names (organisations, products, codenames, customer aliases) to redact verbatim. Each entry is treated as a case-insensitive, word-boundary-anchored literal — no regex syntax needed. Example: ["AcmeCorp internal", "Project Hydra", "Deal-2026-Q3"].

baton.autosave.enabled.description: Enable context-pressure autosave (flush based on event count + wall clock)

baton.autosave.eventThreshold.description: Trigger autosave once this many captured events are buffered

baton.autosave.minutesThreshold.description: Trigger autosave after this many minutes since the last flush (with any pending events)

baton.healthAlertThreshold.description: Show a warning notification if memory health score falls below this value (0 = disabled)

baton.scope.markdownDescription:
Retrieval scope for stored sessions.

- `user`: all sessions across all workspaces and repos.
- `workspace`: sessions captured in the current VS Code workspace only.
- `repo`: sessions captured against the same git origin URL (works across machines/clones).

baton.validateAgainstCodebase.description: Drop or de-rank retrieved memories whose key files no longer exist or are empty in the current codebase.

baton.freshnessFloor.description: Minimum freshness score (0-1, fraction of key files still present) required for a memory to survive validation. Lower = more lenient.

baton.githubCompatibleMode.markdownDescription: Mirror GitHub Copilot's agentic-memory contract: 28-day retention and repo-scoped retrieval. When enabled, overrides `#baton.retentionDays#` and `#baton.scope#`.

baton.policySource.markdownDescription: Optional remote URL to a validated JSON array of redaction rules. Loaded on startup and after settings changes, then appended to the built-in policy set.

walkthroughs.title: Get started with Baton
walkthroughs.description: Persistent, redacted session memory for GitHub Copilot — set up in four steps.

steps.title: Choose your privacy posture
steps.description:
Run the privacy wizard to lock down capture, snippets, terminal commands, and exports before your first session.
[Run privacy wizard](command:baton.runPrivacyWizard)

steps.title: Open the Sessions view
steps.description:
Baton stores compressed coding sessions in the **Memory** sidebar. Open it to confirm capture is active.
[Open Sessions view](command:workbench.view.extension.baton)

steps.title: Capture your first snapshot
steps.description:
Force-capture the current activity so you can see how compression and redaction work.
[Capture snapshot](command:baton.captureSnapshot)

steps.title: Chat with your memory
steps.description:
Open Copilot Chat and type `@baton /recent` to query past sessions. Try `/search`, `/timeline`, `/azure`, `/health`, or `/export`.
[Open Copilot Chat](command:workbench.action.chat.open)

steps.title: Tune retention & GitHub-compatible mode
steps.description:
Open Settings and adjust **Retention Days**, **Max Stored Sessions**, **Max Store Size MB**, or enable **GitHub Compatible Mode** to mirror Copilot's 28-day repo-scoped contract.
[Open settings](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%22baton%22)

toolSets.name: Baton
toolSets.description: Search and save to Baton persistent session memory

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run check:release:strict && npm run typecheck && npm run bundle:prod
scripts.test: npm run lint && tsc -p ./tsconfig.test.json && node scripts/setup-test-env.js && node scripts/run-tests.mjs
scripts.measure:activation: npm run bundle:prod && tsc -p ./tsconfig.test.json && node scripts/measure-activation.js
scripts.package: npm run vscode:prepublish && vsce package --out baton-mem.vsix
